frameworks.name: compressive temporal summation
frameworks.one_line: subadditive integration of visual responses over time across cortical areas

frameworks.name: population receptive field modeling
frameworks.one_line: spatial pRF approach extended to characterize temporal encoding

frameworks.name: compressive spatial summation
frameworks.one_line: earlier spatial-domain finding paralleled by the present temporal result

frameworks.name: linear systems prediction
frameworks.one_line: linear additive baseline that responses to longer stimuli fail to match

core_question: How do human visual cortical areas integrate visual information distributed over time?
key_assumptions: fMRI signal amplitudes can index temporal processing despite coarse temporal resolution
two simple operations (summation then compressive nonlinearity) suffice across the hierarchy
carefully controlled temporally modulated stimuli can isolate temporal computation
manipulations: visual area along the cortical hierarchy
key_findings: all visual areas show subadditive summation: longer-stimulus responses fall below linear prediction
fMRI responses to two stimuli are reduced for brief interstimulus intervals (adaptation)
these effects are more pronounced in areas anterior to V1-V3
interpretations: temporal encoding across visual cortex is captured by summation followed by a compressive nonlinearity
compressive temporal summation directly parallels compressive spatial summation
limitations_noted: fMRI has coarse temporal resolution relative to neural dynamics
